calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Petition For Settlement Of First Interim Account Or Petition For Final Distribution

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Petition For Settlement Of First Interim Account Or Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-08-2026 Continued To Aug-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
TENTATIVE RULING: Continued to 08/10/2026 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 05/26/2026).
EXAMINER: EDWARD MIYAUCHI

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dylan Hyatt for executor Patricia Brown (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Final Distribution.

The Court noted several outstanding issues previously raised by the probate examiner remained unresolved.

Following discussion, the Court took the petition taken off calendar and directed that a new petition be filed, either for settlement of a first interim account with a request to keep the estate open or for final distribution with appropriate supporting documentation.

The Court set a status hearing regarding filing of a new petition for June 8, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-22-2025 Continued To Feb-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-18-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-14-2025 Continued To Aug-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Berenice Villa, Esq., for Patricia Brown, Personal Representative.

At the outset, the court acknowledges the recently filed declaration. At the court's query, counsel advises that a fiduciary accountant has been obtained to prepare the final account for this matter and anticipates filing a petition for distribution within 90 days. Counsel requests a continuance of 180 days, or as soon thereafter as is suitable to the Court, to allow for completion of the above filing. Counsel is advised that an appropriate method to request to continue Administration is via a formal petition for approval of report of status of Administration, instead of a declaration. Counsel is reminded of Probate Code 12200.

The subject matter is continued to Aug-18-2025 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-11-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Dylan Hyatt, Esq., for and with Patricia Brown, Petitioner.

At the outset, the court acknowledges the probate Examiner's notes, the recently filed supplement, and states that it believes that the 6112(c) presumption has been rebutted. The court confirms that there are no appearances to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated Feb-17-2017 is admitted to probate. Patricia Brown is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $20,000. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Apr-14-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported.
